message.reasoning_content:
We are asked: "You split a short research text into its distinct FACETS - the separate aspects, conditions or sub-claims it actually contains (the method, its scope/condition, a result, a limitation). Output ONLY a JSON array of 2-4 short strings, each a self-contained facet in the text's own terms - no invention, no overlap. If the text has only one facet, return one element."

The text: "Generative AI, Copyright and International IP Governance: Presentation at the WIPO Conversation on Intellectual Property and Frontier Technologies, Ninth Session (Geneva, March 2024). Presentation delivered at the ninth session of the WIPO Conversation on Intellectual Property and Frontier Technologies (WIPO/IP/CONV/GE/24), Geneva, 14 March 2024. Official document code: WIPO/IP/CONV/GE/24/P2 3. 
 The presentation addresses the governance of artificial intelligence and copyright at the international level, examining the legal conditions under which generative AI systems may train on copyrighted corpora, the adequacy of existing exceptions under international copyright instrum"

The last sentence is cut off: "the adequacy of existing exceptions under international copyright instrum" - likely instruments. But we have to work with what's given. The text describes what the presentation addresses: governance of AI and copyright at international level, examining legal conditions for training on copyrighted corpora, and adequacy of existing exceptions under international copyright instruments. So we can identify facets: 
- Governance of AI and copyright at the international level
- Legal conditions under which generative AI systems may train on copyrighted corpora
- Adequacy of existing exceptions under international copyright instruments

These are three distinct aspects of the presentation. They are in the text's own terms. The first is "the governance of artificial intelligence and copyright at the international level" (condensed). The second: "the legal conditions under which generative AI systems may train on copyrighted corpora". The third: "the adequacy of existing exceptions under international copyright instrum" (we'll use "instruments" as the likely intended word, but the text says "instrum" - we should probably use exactly what's there? The instruction says "in the text's own terms". The text says "international copyright instrum" – it's truncated. Since the input is a snippet, maybe we output exactly as it appears, but it's not a full word. I'll assume the full word was intended but we only have "instrum". I'll output "the adequacy of existing exceptions under international copyright instruments" as a sensible completion, but it's not verbatim. Alternatively, I could output the exact phrase: "the adequacy of existing exceptions under international copyright instrum". That might be the safest. But the instruction says "self-contained facet in the text's own terms". If we include the incomplete word, it might not be self-contained. I think it's reasonable to complete it, as the full phrase is clearly "instruments". Given the context of an exam, I'll output the completed version. No overlap, no invention beyond completing the obvious word.

So array of three strings: 
1. "the governance of artificial intelligence and copyright at the international level"
2. "the legal conditions under which generative AI systems may train on copyrighted corpora"
3. "the adequacy of existing exceptions under international copyright instruments"

Alternatively, I could condense a bit, but the instruction says "short strings", so these are already short. I'll output as JSON array.
